Standard Racial Traits

  • Ability Score Modifiers: Sea Orcs are powerful and enduring but tend to lack proper education. They gain +2 Strength,+2 Constitution, and –2 Intelligence.
  • Type: Sea Orcs are humanoids with the orc subtype.
  • Size: Sea Orcs are Medium creatures and thus have no bonuses or penalties due to their size.
  • Base Speed: Sea Orcs have a base speed of 30 feet.
  • Languages: Sea Orcs begin play speaking Common and Orc. Orcs with high Intelligence scores can chose from the following: Aquan, Dwarven, Giant, Gnoll, Goblin, Grippli, Undercommon. See the Linguistics skill page for more information about these languages.

Offensive Racial Traits

  • Weapon Familiarity: Sea Orcs are raised in many fishing communities and are always proficient with Boarding Axes, Boarding Pikes, Sea Knives and Cutlass. They also treat the Harpoon and Man-Catcher as martial weapons.
  • Seaworthy: Sea Orcs treat the swim skill as a class skill with an additional +2 racial bonus. Additionally, they may take a 10 on swim checks (except in stormy water or worse).
  • Powerful Lungs: Sea Orcs can hold their breath much longer than most races, up to 3 rounds per point of their Constitution score. In addition, Sea Orcs take an additional round before succumbing to death by suffocation or drowning.

Senses Racial Traits

  • Darkvision: Orcs can see perfectly in the dark up to 30 feet.

Alternate Racial Traits

  • Navigator: Some Sea Orcs have a natural sense of direction and plot out efficient routes, though their swim training falls to neglect. These Sea Orcs gain a +2 racial bonus to Knowledge (geography) and Survival checks. This trait replaces Seaworthy.
  • Brutal: Sea Orcs whose lineage calls back to their old ways sometimes reawaken in later generations. Stronger but mentally stunted, these Sea Orcs are often given simple yet arduous tasks within the Sea Orc community. Such Sea Orcs gain a +4 to Strength but a -2 to Intelligence, Wisdom, and Charisma. This replaces a Sea Orc's normal ability scores.
  • Feral: Sea Orcs who embrace their natural lust for violence to the point of ignoring the signs of death's approach. Such Sea Orcs gain the Ferocity Trait but take a -2 penalty on Sense Motive and Perception checks to avoid being surprised.
  • High Grounded: Some Sea Orcs who have experienced a traumatic event with water form a strong fear of it and refuse to be near it. Such Sea Orcs always treat the climb skill as a class skill and can always take 10 on such checks in addition a Sea Orc requires only 1 hand to be free to be able to climb. This replaces Sea Worthy
  • Sniffer: Orcs with this racial trait gain a limited scent ability with half the normal range. This racial trait replaces weapon familiarity.
  • Squalid: Some Sea Orcs exist in surroundings so filthy and pestilent that even other Sea Orcs would have difficulty living with them. Sea Orcs with this trait gain a +2 racial bonus on saving throws made to resist nausea, the sickened condition, and disease. But take a -2 penalty on all Charisma-based skill checks (excluding Intimidation).
  • Tusks: Some Sea Orcs grow massive tusks reminiscent of their ancient ancestors. These Sea Orcs gain a primary Gore natural attack (1d6). This trait replaces Powerful Lungs.

contextNotes.text: Can take a 10 on swim checks (except in stormy water or worse)
